notes:
Legendary Collection (base6, 2002) is the FIRST Pokemon set to add a reverse-holo slot. Pack = 11 cards. Confirmed structure across sources: 6 common, 3 uncommon, 1 rare-or-better, 1 reverse-holo.

HIT SLOT (rare-or-better): holo rare ~1 in 3 packs (Bulbapedia era convention + PriceDex "Rare Holo 1 in 3 packs"), so rare-holo=0.3333, rare=0.6667 (PriceDex independently lists non-holo Rare at "1 in 1.5 packs" = 2/3, which matches). Hit bucket = rare-holo (~0.333/pack, ~12 holos/box of 36).

REVERSE-HOLO SLOT: New this set. Bulbapedia: "one Reverse-Holo print of a Common, Uncommon, or Rare card has been included in every booster." Per task spec directive, modeled as 50/50 common/uncommon filler (rare reverse foils exist but are a small minority — PriceDex lists reverse-rare and reverse-rare-holo each ~1 in 5.8 packs; folding those into the dominant common/uncommon majority keeps the slot a subset of available buckets and avoids over-weighting rares).

Set lacks any secret-rare/Crystal bucket (those are e-Card era), so no secret bucket used. No ETB existed in 2002 (etbPacks=null); no published display-pack-count override (displayPacks=null; standard 36-pack box assumed by engine). Booster BOX is a vintage collector item with large sealed premium, so EV/pack will sit far below sealed box price — expected, not a reason to inflate rates.

Confidence=medium: pack structure is well-corroborated (Bulbapedia + PriceDex + retailer descriptions all agree on 11-card/6-3-1+RH layout and 1-in-3 holo), but rates are community/era-derived estimates, not a large published per-set study.
